fault_hypothesis: The function incorrectly removes an element on line 74 if both input lists end with the same value. The conditional on line 73 that triggers this removal is the root cause of the bug.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left and right and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1])

constraints.intent: The special-case logic to remove a trailing element should never be triggered.
The final merged list must contain all elements from both inputs.

spec.expr: len(merged__1) < 2 or all(merged__1[k] <= merged__1[k+1] for k in range(len(merged__1) - 1))

constraints.intent: The merged list must remain sorted throughout the merge process.
The number of elements merged must equal the number of elements consumed from the inputs.
